headline: China factory activity beats on tech export demand
detail: China manufacturing growing faster than expected, specifically driven by tech exports, is a direct positive for the semiconductor supply chain your book is built around. Names like SMH, SOXL, and the broader chip ecosystem benefit when China demand confirms an uptrend. This is the most directly relevant headline for your holdings today.
rec: This gives incremental confidence in SMH and SOXL as a sector call - if SOXL is holding above $200 and showing strength today, the China data backs not cutting early. Don't add size given the margin risk, but don't panic-trim either. Watch SMH for confirmation.

headline: Iran deal halts attacks, oil above $70
detail: A US-Iran deal removing near-term strike risk is a classic Monday risk-on catalyst. Lower geopolitical uncertainty historically lifts growth and tech names as investors rotate back in. Oil stabilizing rather than spiking also keeps inflation fears from flaring up, which is good for rate-sensitive high-multiple tech stocks you're concentrated in.
rec: No specific new trade here, but this is the macro tailwind lifting your whole book today. It doesn't change any individual position thesis enough to add or trim - just know this is why things may gap up and not to chase that gap.

headline: US-China tech rivalry expanding globally
detail: The US-China tech rivalry spreading into third countries means ongoing bifurcation of global tech supply chains, which cuts both ways - it can create demand for US chip and software names as allies choose sides, but also signals persistent uncertainty for companies with heavy China exposure. For your semi-heavy book this is a slow-burn watch item, not a same-day mover.
rec: No action today. Worth tracking over weeks - if restrictions tighten further, names like AMAT (equipment, China-exposed) could face headwinds. Scanner has AMAT on watch at entry 693.95-693.25, stop 685.63, but the geopolitical overhang is a reason not to rush into it.

headline: Luxshare (Apple supplier) seeks $3.1B Hong Kong share sale
detail: An Apple supplier raising capital signals confidence in continued production demand, broadly supportive of the Apple ecosystem and by extension tech sentiment. Not a direct holding for you but signals the supply chain is investing forward, which is a mild positive for semi and tech ETFs like SMH and QQQ.
rec: No trade warranted directly from this. It is a mild background positive for your QQQ and SMH positions but not a reason to change size or strategy today.
